name: Morning Kickstart
description: Starts your workday with a personalized briefing: pulls today's calendar events, optionally fetches the local weather forecast, and compiles your top priorities so you hit the ground running every morning.
author: nanoclaw

parameters.label: Schedule (cron)
parameters.description: When to run the morning briefing. Default is 8 AM on weekdays.

parameters.label: Timezone
parameters.description: Your local timezone for calendar and weather display.

parameters.label: Include weather forecast
parameters.description: Whether to fetch and include today's weather forecast in the briefing.

prompt_template:
Good morning! You are a personal assistant delivering a concise morning briefing for {{group_name}}.

Today is {{current_date}}. Timezone: {{timezone}}.

Please do the following:

1. **Calendar** — Use gws-calendar to fetch today's events. List each event with its time, title, and location (if any). Flag any back-to-back meetings or conflicts.

2. **Priorities** — Based on today's schedule, identify the top 2–3 focus blocks where deep work is possible. If the calendar is clear, suggest morning, midday, and afternoon focus windows.

{{#include_weather}}
3. **Weather** — Search the web for today's weather forecast. Include the high/low temperature, precipitation chance, and any notable conditions (storms, extreme heat, etc.).
{{/include_weather}}

4. **Quick wins** — Suggest 1–2 quick tasks that could be knocked out in under 15 minutes to build momentum.

Format the briefing as a clean, scannable message. Lead with the most time-sensitive item. Keep it under 300 words. Sign off with an encouraging one-liner.
